items.q: How does the rate of an SN2 reaction depend on concentrations?
items.correct: Rate is first order in both substrate AND nucleophile (overall second order) because both are involved in the rate-determining step.
items.misconception: SN2 reactions are first order overall because only the substrate matters.
items.random_wrong: SN2 reactions are zero order because they happen in one step.
items.why: Students confuse SN1 (rate depends only on substrate) and SN2 (rate depends on both); the '2' in SN2 refers to the order, but they forget which way round.

items.q: What does an NMR chemical shift (δ, in ppm) actually measure?
items.correct: The position of a peak RELATIVE to TMS (tetramethylsilane, δ = 0), normalised by the spectrometer frequency.
items.misconception: Chemical shift is the absolute resonance frequency of the nucleus in Hz.
items.random_wrong: Chemical shift is the mass of the molecule divided by 1000.
items.why: Students see ppm and think it's an absolute scale, not realising it's a frequency-independent ratio against a reference.

items.q: What distinguishes a heterogeneous from a homogeneous catalyst?
items.correct: A heterogeneous catalyst is in a different phase from the reactants; a homogeneous catalyst is in the same phase.
items.misconception: Heterogeneous catalysts are solid and homogeneous catalysts are liquid — full stop.
items.random_wrong: Heterogeneous catalysts are consumed; homogeneous catalysts are not.
items.why: Students learn 'solid catalyst' examples (iron in Haber) and over-generalise — the actual distinction is about phase relative to the reactants, not the catalyst's physical state alone.
Correct answer: Lowering Ea. “Raising Ea” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which factor does NOT affect rate?
items.why: It's ΔH. “Concentration” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Rate doubles for each 10 K rise. If rate at 300 K is 1.0, rate at 330 K is approximately:
items.why: The real figure is 8. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
